result:
Fixed two bugs in `benchmarks/tasks/fix-baseline/orders.js`:

1. **Off-by-one in `calcTotal`** (orders.js:5): the loop ran `i <= items.length`, reading one element past the end of the array. `items[items.length]` is `undefined`, so `undefined.price` threw a TypeError on every call — which also broke `averageItemPrice` and `formatOrder`, since both call `calcTotal`. Changed the condition to `i < items.length`.

2. **Division by zero in `averageItemPrice`** (orders.js:19): with an empty items array it returned `0 / 0 = NaN`. Added a guard that returns `0` for an empty array.

`applyDiscount` and `formatOrder` are correct as-is (unknown discount codes fall through to the undiscounted total). I tried to run a quick smoke test with node but the command wasn't approved in this session — the changes are small enough to verify by inspection, but you can run the file's functions yourself if you want runtime confirmation.

tool_input.description: Run quick smoke test of fixed order functions
Smoke test the fixed order functions
